title: malleastrum longifoliolatum
intro: Malleastrum longifoliolatum is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Rutaceae and commonly known as the 'African bitter orange'. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malleastrum longifoliolatum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, malaria, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of Malleastrum longifoliolatum include tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
